task_description: Create a new code repository with a test name and description, initialized with a README, an Actionscript gitignore template, and an Apache 2.0 license.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The zoomed-in crop shows the center of the Google homepage search box, with a semitransparent red X placed directly on the empty white search field. Beneath it are circular shortcut tiles, including one with a blue sparkle-like icon and one with the black GitHub cat logo. In the full-screen view, this is a Chrome new tab page in Chinese with the Google logo centered above the search bar, shortcut icons below, and the browser toolbar and address bar at the top.
caption.action: Click the empty Google search field in the middle of the page.
caption.expectation: The search box should become active with a text cursor, ready for typing.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The visible text entry area is the centered Google search box, expanded into a white autocomplete panel. The search field contains the text "github" with the text cursor at the end, indicating the query is being entered into the active field rather than replacing highlighted text. Below it, suggestions appear including "github - Google 搜索," a result labeled "GitHub" with the black cat logo, and additional suggestions such as "github copilot," "github desktop," "github login," and "github trending." In the full-screen view, this is the Google homepage in Chrome with the large Google logo above the search box, Gmail and 图片 links in the top right, and the browser toolbar at the top.
caption.action: Type "GITHUB" into the active Google search field.
caption.expectation: The search box should show the completed query and the autocomplete suggestions should update to match GitHub-related searches and results.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X placed on a plain light-gray horizontal band with no visible button, text, icon, input field, or handle under it, indicating a click on a blank region rather than a control. In the full-screen view, Chrome is open on the Google homepage with the query "github" in the central search box and an autocomplete panel expanded beneath it. The suggestions include "github - Google 搜索" and a highlighted suggestion row labeled "GitHub" with the black cat logo. The click target is not on that panel or any toolbar item, but on empty page background outside the suggestions.
caption.action: Click the blank page background outside the autocomplete panel.
caption.expectation: The suggestion list should lose focus or dismiss, leaving the page in a neutral state with the typed query still present in the search field.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered directly on the purple underlined first search result title text, which reads "GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub." This is a clickable search-result link, not a blank region. In the full-screen view, Chrome is displaying Google search results for "github" in Chinese, with the GitHub result at the top of the results list and additional GitHub-related links below it.
caption.action: Click the top GitHub search result link titled "GitHub · Change is constant. GitHub keeps you ahead. · GitHub."
caption.expectation: The browser should navigate from the Google results page to the GitHub website page for that result.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered on the green rounded rectangular button labeled "New," with a small white repository-style icon on its left. This is a clickable UI control in the left sidebar, not a blank area. In the full-screen view, GitHub is open on the Dashboard page. The left sidebar contains a "Top repositories" section with a repository search field and the green "New" button above the repository list. The main area shows the Home feed.
caption.action: Click the green "New" button in the left sidebar under "Top repositories."
caption.expectation: GitHub should open the new repository creation page or dialog.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered inside an empty single-line text field with a blue outline, indicating the active repository name input box. Below it is helper text suggesting a sample name, and above it is the label "Repository name *". In the full-screen view, Chrome is on GitHub's "Create a new repository" page. The form is in the General section, with the owner dropdown to the left of the repository name field, a Description field below, and configuration options such as visibility, README, gitignore, and license further down.
caption.action: Click inside the empty "Repository name *" text field.
caption.expectation: The repository name field should gain input focus, placing the text cursor there so a repository name can be entered.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The text entry area is the single-line field labeled "Repository name *", outlined in blue to show it is active. Inside the field, the current visible text is "test", indicating the repository name is being entered in that box rather than a search field or description area. In the full-screen view, Chrome is on GitHub's "Create a new repository" page, with the owner selector to the left of the repository name field, a green availability check message below, a Description field underneath, and repository configuration options such as visibility, README, .gitignore, and license further down.
caption.action: Type "TEST1" into the active "Repository name *" field, appending it to the existing text.
caption.expectation: The repository name field should update to include the newly typed text, and GitHub should revalidate the name availability beneath the field.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a semitransparent red X centered inside the empty single-line field labeled "Description". The field has a blue outline, indicating it is the active text box. In the full-screen view, Chrome is on GitHub's "Create a new repository" page. The repository name field above contains "test1" with a green availability message, and the Configuration section with visibility, README, .gitignore, and license options appears below.
caption.action: Click inside the empty "Description" text field.
caption.expectation: The description field should receive focus with a text cursor ready for typing, while the rest of the page remains unchanged.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the single-line text box labeled "Description" with a blue outline indicating it is active. The text currently visible inside is "test1 is for tes", showing that a repository description is being entered and the new text is appending to the existing content rather than replacing it. In the full-screen view, Chrome is on GitHub's "Create a new repository" page; the "Repository name *" field above contains "test1" with a green availability message, and the "Configuration" section below shows options such as visibility, README, .gitignore, and license.
caption.action: Type "TEST1 IS FOR TEST" into the active "Description" field, appending it to the existing description text.
caption.expectation: The description field should update to include the newly typed words, and the character count below the field should increase while the rest of the page remains unchanged.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped view shows the full GitHub "Create a new repository" page without any red X, indicating a scroll action rather than a click target. The visible form currently includes the "General" section with the owner dropdown, the "Repository name *" field filled with "test1" and a green availability message, and the "Description" field containing "test1 is for test" with a blue outline. Below that, the top of the "Configuration" section is visible, including "Choose visibility," "Add README," ".gitignore," and "Add license." In the full-screen view, this is the Chrome browser on github.com/new, and the page scrollbar indicates there is more content below, so the page is ready to be moved downward to reveal additional repository settings.
caption.expectation: The repository form should move upward, revealing more of the lower configuration options and content beneath the currently visible settings.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the small toggle switch in the "Add README" row, with the semitransparent red X centered on the switch knob and the label "Off" visible to its left. This is a UI control in the repository configuration area. The full-screen view shows Chrome on GitHub's "Create a new repository" page, with the Configuration section visible, including visibility set to Public, the Add README toggle, and dropdowns for .gitignore and license.
caption.action: Click the "Add README" toggle switch that is currently set to "Off."
caption.expectation: The toggle should switch on immediately, indicating a README will be added to the new repository.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the full GitHub page rather than a tightly zoomed target, with no semitransparent red X, indicating a scroll action. The visible form is the "Create a new repository" page with the repository name set to "test1," the description field containing "test1 is for test," and the Configuration section showing visibility set to Public, the Add README toggle switched on, .gitignore set to "No .gitignore," and license set to "No license." Near the bottom, the heading "Jumpstart your project with Copilot (optional)" is partially visible, suggesting more content lies below. The full-screen image confirms this is Chrome on github.com/new and the page scrollbar on the right is positioned so the page can be moved further downward.
caption.expectation: The page should move upward, revealing more of the lower repository setup options below the currently visible Configuration and Copilot sections.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the gray dropdown control labeled "No .gitignore" with a small downward chevron on its right; the semitransparent red X is centered over this dropdown, indicating the click target is this UI control. The full-screen image shows GitHub's "Create a new repository" page in Chrome, within the Configuration section below the enabled "Add README" toggle and above the "No license" dropdown.
caption.action: Click the "No .gitignore" dropdown in the Configuration section.
caption.expectation: A selection menu should open for choosing a .gitignore template, allowing the current "No .gitignore" value to be changed.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows an open .gitignore template dropdown panel with a search field at the top and a vertical list of templates below. A semitransparent red X is centered on the highlighted list item labeled "Actionscript," indicating that exact option in the menu is the click target. The full-screen image shows GitHub's "Create a new repository" page in Chrome, in the Configuration section under "Add .gitignore," with the dropdown expanded over the page and other options like "No .gitignore," "AL," "Ada," and "AdventureGameStudio" visible nearby.
caption.action: Click the "Actionscript" option in the open .gitignore template dropdown menu.
caption.expectation: The dropdown menu should close and the .gitignore field should update from "No .gitignore" to "Actionscript," indicating the template has been selected.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the gray dropdown button labeled "No license" with a small downward chevron on its right; a semitransparent red X is centered directly on this button, marking it as the click target. Above it, the ".gitignore" field already shows "Actionscript." The full-screen image shows GitHub's "Create a new repository" page in Chrome, in the Configuration section beneath the enabled "Add README" toggle and the selected ".gitignore" template.
caption.action: Click the "No license" dropdown button in the Configuration section.
caption.expectation: The license selection menu should open, showing available license options to choose from.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows an open license dropdown titled "Choose a license" with a search box labeled "Search licenses" at the top and a list of options below. A semitransparent red X is centered directly on the list item labeled "Apache License 2.0," indicating that exact menu option is the target. The full-screen image shows GitHub's "Create a new repository" page in Chrome, in the Configuration section, with the "Add license" dropdown expanded beneath the already selected ".gitignore" value "Actionscript."
caption.action: Click the "Apache License 2.0" option in the open license dropdown menu.
caption.expectation: The license menu should close and the "Add license" field should update from "No license" to "Apache License 2.0," indicating the license has been selected.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows the full GitHub "Create a new repository" page with no red X, indicating a scroll action rather than a specific target. Visible sections include "General" with the owner dropdown, repository name field containing "test1," and description text, followed by "Configuration" showing visibility set to Public, the Add README toggle turned on, ".gitignore" set to "Actionscript," and license set to "Apache License 2.0." Near the lower part of the view, the next section titled "Jumpstart your project with Copilot (optional)" is partially visible, including the beginning of the "Prompt" area. The full-screen image confirms the browser is on github.com/new and that the page content is positioned around the middle, so scrolling down would move the form further toward lower sections.
caption.action: Scroll down on the page to move further below the Configuration section toward the lower repository setup options.
caption.expectation: The page should shift upward, revealing more of the sections below, such as additional Copilot prompt content and lower repository creation options.

caption.observation: Cropped image shows The cropped image shows a green rounded rectangular button labeled "Create repository" with a semitransparent red X centered on it, indicating the exact click target is this button-shaped UI control. The full-screen image shows the lower portion of GitHub's "Create a new repository" page in Chrome, with repository settings already filled in above, the optional Copilot prompt box visible, and the green "Create repository" button positioned below that form area near the bottom of the page.
caption.expectation: The button should activate and GitHub should submit the form, leading to repository creation and navigation to the new repository page or a loading transition.
